title: Weekly Insights Review
summary:
Read here everything you need to know


March had a better feeling in the spot market
"Looking forward towards the seasonally strongest period of Q2 and Q3, market sentiment remains rather mixed, anticipating a few generous spikes but being pensive for the annual average performance." Read More

What are the immediate areas of risk we should be focusing on? In my nearly 20 years in investments there's one rule that seems to work under any circumstance: Warren Buffet's "Only when the tide goes out do you discover who's been swimming naked." Read More

Dirty tanker rates are already lofty and even more upside is expected for the remainder of the year
Numerous structural and seasonal factors are pointing to higher crude prices in the coming months, and onshore crude inventories are already drawing right now. Read More

Snapshot of Spot Freight Rates, Supply-Demand Trends, Port Congestions
In the first week of April, there were signs of firmness in the larger vessel segments, while there was some weakening in Supramax and Handysize vessels.

children.summary: The European Union embargo on Russian petroleum products took effect on 5 February and is based on the $60 oil price cap introduced on 5 December by the major G7 (Group of Seven) countries.
